channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Cell service getting worse? One study suggests that it is
item.description: The study, from J.D. Power, showed an increase in reported wireless network problems between the first and the second half of 2024.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, NewsNation

item.title: Beyoncé, Kendrick, Sabrina and more: AP writers predict who will win at the 2025 Grammys
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Beyoncé leads the 2025 Grammys nominee pack, but Billie Eilish, Kendrick Lamar and Charli xcx are vying for their moment in the spotlight. Beyoncé is up for 11 awards thanks to “Cowboy Carter,” tying Michael Jackson’s “Thriller” record for most nominations for a single album.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN and JONATHAN LANDRUM JR., Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: East Palestine resident: Town 'fractured' years after derailment
item.description: The East Palestine community is still "fractured" two years after the train derailment, a resident says.
item.dc:creator: Marni Hughes, NewsNation

item.title: North Carolina troopers under investigation, about 180 traffic cases dismissed
item.description: District Attorney Lorrin Freeman said she "concluded that there were statements that were made by that trooper that appear to be false and misleading.
item.dc:creator: Mary Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to sign executive order banning transgender people from military service
item.description: A fact sheet for Monday’s executive order refers to transgender Americans as “trans-identifying,” a technical term used by some to delegitimize transgender identities.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Auschwitz survivors warn of rising hatred on the 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation
item.description: OSWIECIM, Poland (AP) — Auschwitz survivors warned Monday of the rising antisemitism and hatred which they are witnessing in the modern world as they gathered with world leaders and European royalty on the 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What to know about Trump's first executive actions on climate and environment
item.description: President Donald Trump's first week in office included a flurry of executive orders with implications for Earth's climate and environment.
item.dc:creator: MELINA WALLING, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Person seen firing gun into air amid crowd of Philadelphia Eagles fans; police investigating
item.description: Video of the alleged incident, shared to X late on Sunday, appeared to show a large crowd celebrating the Eagles' win when a person raised the gun into the air.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Live updates: Trump to sign executive order eliminating DEI from military, meet House GOP at Florida retreat
item.description: President Trump is set to address the House Republicans at their annual policy retreat in Florida, while the Senate votes on the confirmation of his Treasury Department nominee, Scott Bessent.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Earthquake off coast of Maine shakes Northeast
item.description: A magnitude 3.8 earthquake off the coast of Maine shook parts of the Northeast on Monday morning, with residents as far away as Boston feeling the rumble, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DEA: Tren de Aragua gang 'moved around' after Aurora scrutiny
item.description: A raid of a makeshift nightclub tied to the Venezuelan Tren de Aragua gang could be evidence the gang is moving around.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, NewsNation

item.title: Is cell service getting worse? One study suggests that it is
item.description: The study, from J.D. Power, showed an increase in reported wireless network problems between the first and the second half of 2024.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Connecticut residents report feeling Maine earthquake
item.description: Some people in Connecticut have reported feeling an earthquake recorded off the coast of Maine Monday morning, according to data from the United States Geological Survey.
item.dc:creator: Bailey Wright, NewsNation

item.title: Missing 12-year-old child's mom reveals he never made it to friend's house
item.description: As Virginia Beach Police have deployed a massive search for Juan Sebastian Meija Acevedo, the mother of the missing 12-year-old told her side to WAVY 10. She revealed to 10 On Your Side that her son never made it to his friend's house.
item.dc:creator: Karina F. Garcia, NewsNation

item.title: Rain helps Southern California firefighters but sends ash and mud flowing down hillsides
item.description: Rain is easing after Southern California’s first significant storm of the season brought weekend downpours that aided firefighters but caused ash, mud and debris to flow across streets in wildfire-burned areas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tax season begins Monday: How to file taxes online for free if you made $84K or less
item.description: There are programs available to help people who made $84K or less last year file their federal taxes for free.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Clarke, Local 4

item.title: Texas boy, 12, facing charges after leading deputies in pursuit in stolen vehicle, sheriff's office says
item.description: A 12-year-old boy was charged after leading deputies in a chase while driving a stolen vehicle in Harris County, Texas, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arrest quotas for ICE officers 'a bad idea': William Enyart
item.description: Enacting a daily arrest quota for ICE agents would be "asking for an abuse of their police powers," Ret. U.S. Army Major Gen. William Enyart said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: ICE agents carry out operation in San Jose, mayor says
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (KRON) -- U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) were in San Jose conducting an operation on Sunday, Mayor Matt Mahan's office confirmed in a statement sent to KRON4. The San Jose Police Department was notified that ICE agents were in the city.
item.dc:creator: Jack Molmud, NewsNation

item.title: Ryan Pressly waives no-trade clause to join the Chicago Cubs, AP source says
item.description: The Chicago Cubs are finalizing a trade for Ryan Pressly, adding the right-hander to the back end of their bullpen in their second major offseason deal with the Houston Astros.
item.dc:creator: JAY COHEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Haitian migrants in Springfield, Ohio, turn to faith amid deportation fears
item.description: Despite legally migrating to the U.S. under the country’s Temporary Protected Status program, many of the thousands of Haitians in Springfield, Ohio, are fearful President Donald Trump’s promised migration crackdown will soon include them.
item.dc:creator: Luis Andres Henao And Jessie Wardarski, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: ICE begins 'enhanced targeted operations' in Austin
item.description: A series of ICE raids were conducted in Austin on Sunday, according to sources from the Drug Enforcement Administration.
item.dc:creator: Esmeralda Zamora, NewsNation

item.title: Ohio State celebrates national championship run amid change and farewells
item.description: Fans braved 30-degree temperatures to welcome home a team that beat five top-10 rivals on its way to the title, including Oregon in the Rose Bowl, Texas in the Cotton Bowl and Notre Dame in the College Football Playoff championship game.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE KRAFT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Husband of nurse, mom of three found dead at bottom of staircase went for beer after finding body: police
item.description: Authorities are investigating the death of a Utah nurse, whose husband allegedly told police he went out to get beer after finding her body at the bottom of a stairwell.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Acting deputy AG head visits Chicago to 'observe' immigration enforcement
item.description: Chicago residents, especially in immigrant circles, have already been on edge for months in anticipation of large-scale immigration arrests promised by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Freighter on the move after it was freed from ice on frozen Lake Erie
item.description: BUFFALO, N.Y. (AP) — A freighter was on its way to Canada on Sunday after it was freed from ice that trapped it in a frozen Lake Erie for days, the U.S. Coast Guard said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What is a migrant? What is ICE? 10 terms to help you understand the debate over immigration
item.description: Understanding the many different immigration terms – some technical, some not – can help people better understand immigration news.
item.dc:creator: Ernesto Castañeda Professor of Latin American and Latino studies, American University, Fox 5

item.title: Oklahoma bill would make it a crime punishable by jail time to "possess" a shopping cart belonging to a business
item.description: An Oklahoma lawmaker is proposing a law that would make it a crime punishable by up to a year in jail for anyone caught “in possession of” a shopping cart taken from a store.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Humphrey/KFOR, Fox 5

item.title: Your wages will stretch the furthest in these major US metros, study finds
item.description: A new study by finance site Bankrate found that, after adjusting for the cost of living, the average worker's buying power only grew (compared to the other cities) in a handful of large metros.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Washington state woman is charged in the fatal shooting of a Border Patrol agent in Vermont
item.description: A Washington state woman has been charged in the fatal shooting of a U.S. Border Patrol agent in Vermont, which happened days after authorities began watching her and a German companion who also died in the roadside shootout, the FBI said Friday.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: North Dakota’s overturned abortion ban won’t be in effect during appeal, court rules
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — North Dakota’s abortion ban will not be enforced while the state appeals an earlier decision that found it unconstitutional, the state's highest court ruled Friday. That appeal has yet to fully play out in the state Supreme Court after a judge struck down the law in September.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Costco stands by its DEI policies as other companies scale back
item.description: Costco is pushing back on a shareholder proposal that urges the wholesale club operator to conduct an evaluation of any business risks posed by its diversity, equity and inclusion practices. Investors were expected to vote on the recommendation during the company's annual meeting Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
